name: Universidad Complutense de Madrid
description: One of the oldest universities in the world and the largest in Spain, the Complutense traces its origins to 1499. Its sprawling Ciudad Universitaria campus in Madrid houses 26 faculties and draws students from over 100 countries, making it a cornerstone of Spanish academic life.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid
description: Established in 1968 as a research-intensive institution, the UAM is consistently ranked among Spain's top universities. Its 750-hectare campus north of Madrid hosts 8 faculties with particular strength in sciences, humanities, and medicine.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Madrid
description: Spain's largest and most prestigious technical university, the UPM excels in engineering, architecture, and applied sciences. Its 14 schools across Madrid maintain strong ties to industry and produce a large share of Spain's engineers and architects.

name: Universidad Carlos III de Madrid
description: Founded in 1989, UC3M is known for its internationally oriented programmes and ranks among the best young universities worldwide. All bachelor's degrees are available in English, attracting a large international student body to its three campuses.

name: IE University
description: IE University is a globally ranked private institution renowned for its business and law programmes. Drawing students from over 140 nationalities, it fosters an entrepreneurial mindset and is consistently listed among Europe's most innovative universities.

name: Universidad Rey Juan Carlos
description: One of Madrid's newer public universities, the URJC was established in 1996 and rapidly grew into a large institution with six campuses across the region. It offers broad programmes in communication, health sciences, engineering, and economics.

name: Universidad de Barcelona
description: Founded in 1450, the University of Barcelona is one of Catalonia's leading research institutions and one of the oldest in Spain. With 18 faculties covering virtually every discipline, it is a cultural and academic landmark in the heart of the city.

name: Universidad Autónoma de Barcelona
description: Located on a self-contained campus 30 km from Barcelona, the UAB is one of Spain's top research universities. Founded in 1968, it hosts 40,000 students and is especially strong in life sciences, social sciences, and veterinary medicine.

name: Universidad Pompeu Fabra
description: A compact, selective public university in Barcelona, the UPF has rapidly built a reputation for academic excellence since its founding in 1990. With only 14,000 students, it achieves outstanding results in law, economics, communications, and biomedical research.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Cataluña
description: Catalonia's main technical university, the UPC educates 30,000 students across nine campuses in engineering, architecture, and computer science. It is a leading European research centre and partners with hundreds of companies for technology transfer and innovation.

name: ESADE Business School
description: Part of Universitat Ramon Llull, ESADE is one of Europe's most prestigious business and law schools, consistently ranked in the global top 10 for MBA programmes. Founded in Barcelona in 1958, it draws high-achieving students from over 80 countries.

name: Universidad de Valencia
description: One of Spain's oldest universities, founded in 1499, the UV is the main higher-education institution in the Valencia region. Its three campuses host 50,000 students across all major disciplines and it is renowned for its research in sciences and medicine.

name: Universidad Politécnica de Valencia
description: One of Spain's leading technical universities, the UPV hosts 37,000 students in engineering, architecture, fine arts, and business. Its main campus in Valencia is a hub for innovation and technology transfer, with strong connections to the regional industrial sector.

name: Universidad de Sevilla
description: Founded in 1505, the University of Seville is one of Spain's oldest and most prestigious institutions and the largest university in Andalusia. Its historic buildings in central Seville house 70,000 students across a full spectrum of academic disciplines.

name: Universidad Pablo de Olavide
description: Founded in 1997, the UPO offers a modern educational model on a vibrant self-contained campus in Seville. With 12,000 students, it focuses on social sciences, law, sport science, and humanities, and is known for its international outlook and active campus life.

name: Universidad de Granada
description: Established in 1531, the University of Granada is one of Spain's great historic universities and a leading destination for international students, particularly for its Spanish language and culture programmes. Its campuses spread across the beautiful Moorish city of Granada.

name: Universidad de Salamanca
description: One of the world's oldest universities, founded in 1218, the University of Salamanca is a UNESCO World Heritage candidate city's crown jewel. It is world-famous for its Spanish language programmes and its stunning Renaissance architecture draws thousands of international students each year.

name: Universidad del País Vasco (UPV/EHU)
description: The UPV/EHU is the main public university of the Basque Country, offering courses in both Spanish and Basque across three campuses in Bilbao, San Sebastián, and Vitoria-Gasteiz. With 45,000 students it covers all disciplines and is a key driver of Basque research and innovation.

name: Universidad de Deusto
description: Founded by the Jesuits in Bilbao in 1886, the Universidad de Deusto is one of Spain's most respected private universities. Its riverside campus in Bilbao hosts 11,000 students with particular strengths in law, business, and social sciences, and a significant number of English-language programmes.

name: Universidad de Navarra
description: Founded in 1952, the Universidad de Navarra is one of Spain's foremost private research universities. Located in Pamplona, it is particularly renowned for its medical school and IESE Business School, which is consistently ranked among the world's best MBA programmes.

name: Universidad de Zaragoza
description: One of the oldest universities in Spain, founded in 1542, the University of Zaragoza serves 29,000 students across five campuses in Aragon. It excels in engineering, science, and medicine, and maintains strong links to the region's automotive and renewable energy industries.

name: Universidad de Málaga
description: Founded in 1972, the University of Málaga has grown into a major institution benefiting from the city's emergence as a Mediterranean tech hub. Its 35,000 students study across all major disciplines, and the university has strong industry ties with Málaga's booming tech and innovation sector.

name: Universidad de Alicante
description: Established in 1979 on the sunny Mediterranean coast, the University of Alicante is known for its modern campus and international atmosphere. Its 26,000 students benefit from strong programmes in economics, law, and engineering, and a large international student community.

name: Universidad de Murcia
description: With roots dating back to 1272, the University of Murcia is one of Spain's oldest institutions. The central campus in the city hosts 37,000 students and the university is known for its strength in health sciences, law, and agriculture, reflecting the region's economic profile.

name: Universidad de Oviedo
description: Founded in 1608, the University of Oviedo is the main public university of Asturias with campuses in Oviedo, Gijón, and Mieres. Its 23,000 students study across engineering, law, medicine, and humanities, and the university has deep roots in the region's mining and industrial heritage.

name: Universidad de A Coruña
description: Established in 1989, the University of A Coruña serves Galicia's Atlantic coast from campuses in A Coruña and Ferrol. Its 16,000 students have particular access to strong programmes in naval architecture, engineering, and law, reflecting the city's maritime heritage.
